method: For each cluster produced on 5 real-commit replays from `replay-real-fileGraph.jsonl`, hand-compared the agent's rationale text against the actual git diff. Graded each as correct/partial/wrong. Sample: 21 clusters across 5 commits, all cascades from 31 to 1,025 stories. Replays used signature prompt + claude-sonnet-4-6 + effort=low.

perCommit.subject: Fix: keep original onAllStatusChange timing behavior
perCommit.diffSummary: Adds `await api.setIndex(index)` and `fullAPI.setRef(...)` loop after `recomputeStatusFilter` in stories.ts
perCommit.notes: All rationales correctly identify the new setIndex/setRef calls and the transitive vs direct nature of the affected stories. The catch-all bucket is honest about its catch-all nature.

perCommit.subject: fix(manager-api): await recompute filter calls
perCommit.notes: Direct rationale ('async fix to recomputeTagsFilter/recomputeStatusFilter now being properly awaited') matches the diff verbatim. Other rationales correctly distinguish first-order vs second-order vs catch-all.

perCommit.subject: fix(sidebar): only show Clear button when review filter active
perCommit.diffSummary: Removes $isActive prop from Wrapper styled-component; conditionally renders Clear button via isReviewActive instead of CSS opacity
perCommit.notes: Most technically precise of the five. Cluster rationale literally cites 'the Wrapper styled component no longer accepts $isActive, removing the prop-driven opacity CSS' — exactly what the diff does.

perCommit.subject: Add story
perCommit.diffSummary: Adds WithCTAInactive story export using internal_ctaStatusStore + CHANGE_DETECTION_STATUS_TYPE_ID + newStatusStoryIds
perCommit.partialReasoning: sidebar-refs-stories rationale claims 'the module-level addition of a new export can shift module evaluation order and shared store state.' Technically true but speculative; the real reason is just 'same story file.'

perCommit.subject: test(sidebar): align Sidebar.stories with ToggleButton role=switch
perCommit.diffSummary: Changes role: 'button' → 'switch' and aria-pressed → aria-checked in play functions of StatusesNew, StatusesModified, WithCTAActive, CTAToggleUpdatesLive
perCommit.notes: All three rationales correctly cite the role-attribute change and which stories' play functions would break.

conclusion: 95.2% rationale-correct rate — comfortably above the 80% threshold Page 4 set for 'suppress rationales in the UI.' The agent's rationales reliably cite real diff details (function names, prop names, role attributes), not hallucinated 'plausible-sounding but wrong' descriptions. Trust UX can show the rationale directly to users. The single partial label is a stretch claim about module evaluation order, not a hallucinated diff detail.
